title: Missing check for empty payload in text message validation
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c:980-981

text_packet_supported checks for empty payload:
  if (packet->payload == NULL || packet->payload_len == 0U ||

But in parse_data (lines 724-791), payload defaults to NULL and payload_len defaults to 0 (from memset at line 798).

If a packet has portnum=TEXT_MESSAGE_APP but no DATA_PAYLOAD_FIELD, then:
- payload = NULL
- payload_len = 0
- has_portnum = true
- kind = TEXT_MESSAGE_APP (set at line 767)

Then in dispatch_packet, text_packet_supported returns false (correctly rejecting empty text).

However, the packet reaches dispatch_packet with kind=TEXT_MESSAGE_APP, triggering text_packet_count++ before the supported check. This inflates the text_packet_count stat for invalid packets.

Consider setting kind=MALFORMED for empty TEXT_MESSAGE payloads in parse_data.
notes: Fixed by setting kind=MALFORMED for empty TEXT payloads in parse_data (lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c:624). Commit: a93f618
